message.content: {"units":[{"unit_key":"unit-001","failure_analysis":"Cannot prove: missing lemmas that the only non-intersecting path tuple for the Dyck endpoints is identity-matched, and that each such path has weight 1.","repair_strategy_note":"Use lgv_nonpermutable + catalanHankelMatrix_eq_pathWeightMatrix, then prove a same-unit helper that nipatWeightSum equals 1 by showing the non-intersecting set is a singleton of identity paths with unit weight.","selector_confidence":0.9,"selected_visible_context":[{"context_kind":"prior_project","name_or_label":"LGV.catalanHankelMatrix_eq_pathWeightMatrix","why_needed":"converts Hankel det to path-weight matrix det"},{"context_kind":"prior_project","name_or_label":"LGV.lgv_nonpermutable","why_needed":"reduces det to nipatWeightSum for x-decreasing/y-increasing endpoints"},{"context_kind":"prior_project","name_or_label":"LGV.dyck_paths_shifted_eq_catalan","why_needed":"relates shifted Dyck paths to Catalan numbers"},{"context_kind":"local_file_predecessor","name_or_label":"LGV.translatePath_injective","why_needed":"injectivity may help show uniqueness of paths"}],"same_unit_helper_plan":[{"fresh_name_hint":"nipat_identity_unique","role":"lemma","statement_sketch":"For the source vertices A_i = (-2i,0) and target vertices B_j = (2j,0), the only non-intersecting path tuple is the identity tuple where path i goes from A_i to B_i.","why_needed":"needed to show nipatWeightSum is a singleton sum","depends_on":["LGV.lgv_nonpermutable","LGV.dyckDigraph","LGV.translatePath_injective"],"needed_checked_ingredients":["SimpleDigraph.Path.start","SimpleDigraph.Path.finish","PathTuple.IsNonIntersecting","nipatWeightSum"]},{"fresh_name_hint":"dyck_path_unit_weight","role":"lemma","statement_sketch":"For any Dyck path p in dyckDigraph, the weight under dyckUnitArcWeight equals 1.","why_needed":"each path in the identity tuple has weight 1, so the product is 1","depends_on":["LGV.dyckUnitArcWeight"],"needed_checked_ingredients":["ArcWeight","pathWeight","dyckUnitArcWeight"]}],"needed_mathlib_context":[{"name_or_query":"Finset.sum_singleton","expected_signature_or_shape":"∑ x ∈ {a}, f x = f a","why_needed":"to evaluate nipatWeightSum over a singleton set"},{"name_or_query":"Equiv.refl","expected_signature_or_shape":"Equiv.refl α : α ≃ α","why_needed":"identity permutation for lgv_nonpermutable"}],"missing_or_uncertain_context":["Definition of nipatWeightSum and its computation for singleton sets","PathTuple.IsNonIntersecting predicate 1) k","why_needed":"connects negative upper index to standard binomial coefficient","depends_on":["Ring.choose"],"needed_checked_ingredients":["Ring.choose","Nat.choose","neg_add","sub_eq_add_neg"]},{"fresh_name_hint":"coeff_one_sub_X_pow_neg","role":"lemma","statement_sketch":"coeff k ((1 - X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n)) = Ring.choose (n + k - 1) k","why_needed":"gives explicit coefficient for (1-X)^{-n} expansion","depends_on":["fpsPow_one_add_smul_X'","generalizedNewtonBinomial","coeff_rescale","Ring_choose_neg_mul_neg_one_pow"],"needed_checked_ingredients":["coeff_rescale","fpsPow_one_add_smul_X'"]}],"needed_mathlib_context":[{"name_or_query":"Ring.choose_natCast","expected_signature_or_shape":"Ring.choose (n : K) k = Nat.choose n k for n : ℕ","why_needed":"connects Ring.choose to standard binomial coefficients"},{"name_or_query":"Nat.choose","expected_signature_or_shape":"Nat.choose : ℕ → ℕ → ℕ","why_needed":"standard binomial coefficient for natural numbers"},{"name_or_query":"coeff_mul","expected_signature_or_shape":"coeff n (f * g) = ∑ i ∈ range (n+1), coeff i f * coeff (n-i) g","why_needed":"convolution formula for product of power series"},{"name_or_query":"coeff_rescale","expected_signature_or_shape":"coeff n (rescale a f) = a^n * coeff n f","why_needed":"coefficient extraction after rescaling by -1"}],"missing_or_uncertain_context":["fpsPow_one_add_smul_X' lemma statement and conditions","binomialSeries_coeff for direct coefficient extraction","Ring.choose properties for negative first argument"],"do_not_use_identifiers":[]},{"unit_key":"unit-004","failure_analysis":"Compile failure: Finset.sum_pow expands to sum over Sym (Fin d) n, not Fin n → Fin d. The generated code incorrectly used congr with funext expecting Sym = function type.","repair_strategy_note":"Use the correct return type of Finset.sum_pow: it expands (∑ a ∈ s, f a)^n = ∑ x ∈ s.sym n, ∏ i ∈ range n, f (x.getI i). Need to relate s.sym n to Fin n → s via a bijection, or use a different expansion lemma.","selector_confidence":0.9,"selected_visible_context":[{"context_kind":"prior_project","name_or_label":"AlgebraicCombinatorics.SubtractiveMethods.toSign","why_needed":"sign function used in the sum"},{"context_kind":"prior_project","name_or_label":"AlgebraicCombinatorics.SubtractiveMethods.signProduct","why_needed":"product of signs in the goal"},{"context_kind":"prior_project","name_or_label":"AlgebraicCombinatorics.SubtractiveMethods.signProduct_eq_prod_pow","why_needed":"relates signProduct to product of powers"}],"same_unit_helper_plan":[{"fresh_name_hint":"sum_pow_to_fun_fin","role":"lemma","statement_sketch":"For a finite type α and f : α → ℤ, (∑ a : α, f a)^n = ∑ x : Fin n → α, ∏ i : Fin n, f (x i)","why_needed":"correct expansion of power of sum to functions, avoiding Sym type","depends_on":["Finset.sum_pow","Fintype.piFinset"],"needed_checked_ingredients":["Finset.sum_pow","Fintype.piFinset","Finset.sum_map","Function.Embedding"]}],"needed_mathlib_context":[{"name_or_query":"Finset.sum_pow","expected_signature_or_shape":"(∑ a ∈ s, f a)^n = ∑ x ∈ s.sym n, ∏ i ∈ range n, f (x.getI i)","why_needed":"expansion of power of sum - need to understand actual return type"},{"name_or_query":"Finset.sym","expected_signature_or_shape":"Finset.sym s n : Finset (Sym α n)","why_needed":"multiset of size n from finset s"},{"name_or_query":"Sym","expected_signature_or_shape":"Sym α n = {s : Multiset α // Multiset.card s = n}","why_needed":"understand the type returned by sum_pow"},{"name_or_query":"Fintype.piFinset","expected_signature_or_shape":"Fintype.piFinset t : Finset (∀ a, t a) for family of finsets","why_needed":"finite set of all functions into finite codomain"},{"name_or_query":"Finset.sum_map","expected_signature_or_shape":"∑ x ∈ s.map e, f x = ∑ x ∈ s, f (e x)","why_needed":"convert between different index types for sums"}],"missing_or_uncertain_context":["Exact statement of Finset.sum_pow and its return type","Whether Sym (Fin d) n is equivalent to Fin n → Fin d for the purpose of this sum","Fintype instance for Fin d → ZMod 2 or similar finite function spaces"],"do_not_use_identifiers":[]},{"unit_key":"unit-005","failure_analysis":"Cannot prove: missing definitions for partition transpose, elementary symmetric function e_n as MvPolynomial, skew Schur function s_{λ/μ}, and the first Jacobi-Trudi theorem. The esymm fallback candidates exist in Mathlib but the exact project-specific wrapper is not visible.","repair_strategy_note":"The visible context has hsymmExt for h_n but no esymmExt for e_n, no transpose operation on NPartition, and no skew Schur definition. These are fundamental missing pieces, not just bridge lemmas.","selector_confidence":0.85,"selected_visible_context":[{"context_kind":"prior_project","name_or_label":"SymmetricFunctions.hsymmExt","why_needed":"shows pattern for extending symmetric function to negative indices"},{"context_kind":"prior_project","name_or_label":"SymmetricFunctions.jacobiTrudiMatrixH","why_needed":"matrix construction pattern for first Jacobi-Trudi"},{"context_kind":"prior_project","name_or_label":"SymmetricFunctions.schur","why_needed":"Schur function definition"},{"context_kind":"prior_project","name_or_label":"SymmetricFunctions.NPartition","why_needed":"partition type"}],"same_unit_helper_plan":[],"needed_mathlib_context":[{"name_or_query":"MvPolynomial.esymm","expected_signature_or_shape":"MvPolynomial.esymm σ R n : MvPolynomial σ R for [Fintype σ]","why_needed":"elementary symmetric function as multivariate polynomial"},{"name_or_query":"MvPolynomial.esymm_eq_sum_subtype","expected_signature_or_shape":"esymm σ R n = ∑ t, ∏ i ∈ ↑t, X i","why_needed":"explicit formula for elementary symmetric function"}],"missing_or_uncertain_context":["Definition of NPartition transpose operation","Definition of esymmExt analogous to hsymmExt","Definition of skew Schur function s_{λ/μ}","Statement of first Jacobi-Trudi theorem jacobiTrudi_h","Any duality/omega involution relating h_n and e_n via transpose"],"do_not_use_identifiers":[]}]}
